source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The "Borromaic Rings" have an extraordinary property. Although no two are interlocked, they are strongly connected within each other. If one ring is cut through, the other two fall apart. Which of the following diagrams shows the picture of "Borromaic Rings"?
<image1>
extraction_notes: options field only contains option labels, without option text beyond A-E

image_dependency: question asks which diagram shows the Borromaic Rings, so selecting among visual options is essential
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample cannot be solved from text alone
the defining property involves spatial/topological relationships among rings, which must be read from the diagram
multi_step: solver must apply at least two conditions from the text: no two rings are interlocked, yet all three are jointly linked so cutting one separates the others
the task requires comparing multiple candidate diagrams (5 choices) against the stated property
this is a text-plus-image reasoning task rather than direct visual lookup
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice with a single-letter answer ('B'), giving a clear target for checking
registry_passed=true, with question, image, and answer all present
the stated property provides concrete criteria that can be used to validate whether the selected diagram matches

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present with no registry issues
initial scoring indicates high image dependency and high verifiability, making it a strong visual reasoning candidate
although the answer is choice-only and the task involves topological visual reasoning, the core semantics are intact and recoverable
